data.narrative:
Horizon Quantum Computing is a publicly traded quantum computing company founded by Joe Fitzsimons. The peer universe comprises five same-sector, same-stage public comparables: IonQ ($11.0B), Rigetti Computing ($5.5B), D-Wave Quantum ($2.0B), Infleqtion ($1.8B), and Archer Materials ($60M). This cohort spans a wide valuation range from $60M to $11.0B, reflecting material dispersion in how the market values quantum computing platforms at similar maturity stages.

Peer-median analysis, the applicable triangulation method, yields an implied valuation of $2.0B. This represents the midpoint of the five-company sample and reflects the median approach given the broad range of comparable valuations. The company has raised $131M+ in total capital, including a $110M PIPE completed in December 2025, supporting operations and product development within this valuation context.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.7B to $2.3B, centered on $2.0B. The wide dispersion among public quantum computing peers—spanning nearly two orders of magnitude—constitutes the primary valuation uncertainty. Key drivers of positioning within this range would include technical milestones, qubit count and fidelity roadmaps, customer adoption metrics, and competitive positioning relative to IonQ and Rigetti at the high end of the peer set.
